mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates eliminate discount rate pressure on equity valuations, forcing capital into risk assets and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when UNRATE crosses the 6.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns in >65% of observations during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 235.0 trigger expectations of central bank policy normalization, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the negative correlation breaks down (Rank IC > -0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market strength rather than statistical artifacts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate profit expectations, which immediately prices into equity markets and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during inflationary pressure regimes.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the mechanism loses predictive power outside inflationary regimes.
